title: First trend dataset: breast/mammography, US national + states (reproducible)
context: M1 culminates in publishing the first open trend dataset: breast/mammography 'up-to-date' coverage, US national and states, from accepted public-domain sources (BRFSS, NHIS). This dataset is the project's first concrete open-data deliverable — a reproducible, schema-valid, provenance-covered harmonized dataset that downstream tasks (data dictionary, explainers, dashboard) build on. It must pass statistical and license/provenance review before publication. Population-level aggregate data only; no individual-level records.
objective: Produce and publish the first open trend dataset: breast/mammography 'up-to-date' screening coverage trend, US national + all states, from accepted PD sources, using the pipeline (007) and caveat layer (008). Every series must be schema-valid, provenance-covered, harmonization score >= 90, with basis labeled, break-in-series and suppression flags applied, and small-cell guard enforced. Dataset must be reproducible from committed snapshots in CI.
acceptanceCriteria: Dataset covers breast/mammography 'up-to-date' screening coverage trend for US national and all available states from accepted public-domain sources (BRFSS and/or NHIS); no series from cite-only or excluded sources

Every series is schema-valid per the canonical trend-series schema (task 004): all required fields populated, basis set to self-report or measured as appropriate, denominatorDef stated, provenanceComplete: true
Self-report basis labeled on all applicable series; break-in-series flags applied at survey redesigns or guideline/denominator changes; suppression flags applied for below-threshold cells
Small-cell guard enforced: no published cell below the configured minimum-cell or minimum-denominator threshold; suppressed cells labeled, never imputed
Dataset is reproducible byte-stably from committed snapshots in CI; provenance-coverage gate (100%) and license-status gate both pass
Statistical reviewer and license/provenance reviewer sign-off both recorded before publication
output: A published (committed and CI-green) open trend dataset of breast/mammography 'up-to-date' screening coverage for US national + states: schema-valid, provenance-covered, harmonizationScore >= 90 per series, basis labeled, break-in-series and suppression flags applied, small-cell guard enforced, reproducible from committed snapshots; statistical and license reviewer sign-offs recorded.
